editor_settings.title: The Cult of the Canon G7X: Did Canon Actually Discontinue It?
editor_settings.content:
1. The Short Answer

No, Canon has not discontinued the G7X line. Both the Mark II and Mark III are still officially manufactured and supported by Canon. However, finding one in stock is a completely different story.

2. The Scarcity Explained

The compact camera market was largely declared dead by 2018 due to smartphones. Canon scaled their production facilities accordingly. Then, social media influencers suddenly popularized the "digital camera aesthetic," causing demand to spike by 500% virtually overnight.

@by.diila #canong7x

3. The Used Market Chaos

Because uninformed buyers assume the camera is dead, the used market is currently a disaster. Ten-year-old Mark II bodies are selling for more than they cost brand new. It's an artificial bubble fueled by impatience.

4. How to Actually Buy One

Stop refreshing Amazon. Go directly to Canon's official website and sign up for restock notifications. They quietly list refurbished models frequently. You can also sign up for alerts with major retailers like B&H. The camera exists, you just need a strategy to circumvent the bots.

editor_settings.author: Adam Molina
editor_settings.meta_description: Try buying a brand new Canon G7X from a retail store right now. You'll quickly see why everyone assumes Canon quietly killed their most popular compact camera.
editor_settings.meta_keywords: cameras, cult, canon, canon, actually, discontinue
